content:
A second loop exists beyond triage: wiki → code. Two detection cases:
1. New invariant not yet implemented — the spec has moved ahead of the code. The delta is work.
2. Built but not tested — code exists but no characterisation test freezes its behaviour. Agents can autonomously write these tests because the invariant tells them what correct looks like.

Precondition: invariants in the wiki must be machine-readable (not just prose). The behaviour tree for this loop sits above the triage loop:
Selector
├── [inbox pending?] → triage loop
├── [unimplemented invariants?] → write work item
├── [untested decisions?] → write characterisation test
└── [idle]

provenance.raw_agent: This is the second loop in the system — the first loop is conversation → wiki, this is wiki → code. An agent looking at the wiki and the codebase can detect: 'this invariant is declared but I can't find evidence it's satisfied.' That's a work item.
provenance.raw_user: I'm thinking about what happens when a new invariant enters the spec. It will be implemented or not. There is potentially a delta that is the work that needs doing. Also, If I ask an agent to build something, but I don't add tests, my eventual desire is that this situation is rectified by autonomous agents writing characterisation tests.
